title: The Seven Voices in the Throat

features.name: the sealed mouths
features.desc-on-touch: Seven pressure valves, each one a throat closed on a voice. The archive found them speaking into absolute darkness and chose not to listen further.

features.name: testimony pooling
features.desc-on-touch: The water collects what was meant to stay unrecorded. The archive's refusal has weight; it pools and deepens.

features.name: the sealed valve
features.desc-on-touch: The mechanism that closes at the throat's entrance. Cold metal, still warm from the breath it contains.

features.name: counting marks
features.desc-on-touch: Someone carved tally marks into the stone. The count goes higher than seven. Or perhaps lower—the marks blur in this light.

inscription: seven voices continue speaking into absolute darkness; the archive chose to stop listening at their depth

file.name: Testimony_Sealed_Withheld_Seven_Voices_1987.wav
file.note: Recording cut off at the archive's instruction. The silence contains seven overlapping voices. A fragment of unrelated text was attached once: 'incompleteness as ontological structure'. The origin is

exits.label: back up the valve corridor into gray light
where the throat widens into a cathedral of speaking
toward the tap-tap-tap of smaller voices in the pipes
